subsystem: WF66 GUI/IDE (iGui): Windows MDI-based Direct2D/DirectWrite IDE
purpose: WF66 is a Windows-only IDE that runs Forth inside a Direct2D-rendered MDI frame with cooperative GUI/core integration. The GUI thread owns the Win32 message pump and Direct2D device context; the worker thread owns Wf64Session (the Forth VM) and drains IGuiEvent messages from a cross-thread event mailbox. Key responsibilities: MDI child lifecycle (fedit editor, fconsole REPL, stack_view), event routing (keyboard/mouse → Forth), output capture (console append, stack publish), and render-on-demand (batch-based geometry + text drawing).

arch_os_assumptions.assumption: #![cfg(windows)] — entire igui module is Windows-only; Direct2D/DirectWrite/Win32 APIs not portable
Windows Win32 API imports: HWND, MDICLIENT, WM_* message dispatch, GetMessageW message pump, Win32 message marshaling via SendMessageW/PostMessageW
#![cfg(windows)] — native function exports (iGui.NextEvent, iGui.OpenChild, etc.) use C ABI on x86-64 with i64 register convention
Direct2D-backed render target; HwndRenderTarget tied to HWND lifetime; D2D1_FEATURE_LEVEL_DEFAULT device creation
Direct2D/DirectWrite for text rendering — D2D1HwndRenderTarget, IDWriteTextLayout, DWRITE_FONT_WEIGHT; all tied to Win32 HWND
DXGI_FORMAT_B8G8R8A8_UNORM swap chain, D3D11 → Direct2D composition via HwndRenderTarget
ID2D1DeviceContext (GPU-backed device context) and ID2D1Brush cache; assumes DXGI/D3D11 subsystem is initialized globally by renderer::install()
#[cfg(windows)] on main() entry point; non-Windows builds immediately exit
Windows hardcoded paths (E:\factor\factor.com, E:\NewFactor\factor.image); Factor subprocess with CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag specific to Windows console subsystem
pub mod igui is gated #[cfg(windows)]; NewFactor (lines 74-77) also Windows-only

reusable_asis: Wf64Session core API (pub fn eval, pub fn call_xt, pub fn stack(), pub fn push/pop) — pure Forth engine, no GUI entanglement
channels::IGuiEvent enum — platform-independent event model (keyboard, mouse, focus, resize, menu); serializable to/from i64 tuples
batch::SurfaceCmd — Direct2D command abstraction (FillRect, DrawLine, etc.) with platform-neutral geometry (Rect, Point, Rgba color); could be retargeted to macOS Metal/CoreGraphics
executor::execute(batch) — command interpreter that translates SurfaceCmd → Direct2D calls; logic is D2D-specific but structure is portable
repl_pane::append / fconsole::append — text output accumulation (thread-safe Mutex queues) with PostMessage flushing pattern; can adapt to macOS runloop
stack_view::publish — data-stack visualization model (thread-safe Mutex snapshot) independent of rendering
lib.rs:1650-1750 extern bindings (rt_print_int, rt_dot_s, rt_type, rt_read_line, rt_igui_page, rt_gpane_open, etc.) — C FFI surface that MacNCL IDE can call identically

port_changes.what: IDE window frame + MDI child management
port_changes.where: src/igui/window.rs:592-766 (pub fn run, frame_wnd_proc, MDI frame/client lifecycle)
port_changes.detail: MF66 must replace Win32 HWND/message-pump with macOS NSWindow/NSViewController + event loop integration. MDICLIENT equivalent is a tab bar (NSSplitViewController or custom layout manager) since macOS doesn't have true MDI. WM_USER messages (WM_IGUI_OPEN_CHILD, etc. at window.rs:64-99) become cross-thread channels; SendMessageW → async dispatch queue. RegisterClassExW → NSWindowController subclass. The frame's wallpaper brush (make_logo_brush) → NSView with custom drawRect:.

port_changes.where: src/igui/{renderer.rs, d2d.rs, d3d.rs, dwrite.rs, executor.rs, repl_pane.rs, fconsole.rs, stack_view.rs}
port_changes.detail: Replace D3D11 device + DXGI swap chains with Metal MTLDevice/MTLCommandQueue. Replace Direct2D geometry calls (FillRect, DrawLine, etc.) with MTLRenderCommandEncoder equivalents. Replace DirectWrite text layout with CoreText (NSTextStorage + NSLayoutManager on macOS, or Core Text APIs directly). This touches repl_pane.rs:36-42 (Direct2D/DirectWrite imports), fconsole.rs:36-46 (same), stack_view.rs:23-36 (DXGI format constants). The batch executor (executor.rs:68-251) itself is architecture-independent; only the underlying brush/brush-cache needs retargeting (currently D2D1SolidColorBrush → Metal render state).

channels::IGuiEvent and next_event() are platform-independent; the underlying MPSC queue survives. Replace SendMessageW blocking calls (window.rs:777-830, cp_exports.rs igui_open_child) with async dispatch_async to the GUI thread's dispatch queue. Mac equivalent of PostMessageW → DispatchQueue.main.async { … }. The WM_IGUI_* private message constants become enum variants internally. Timeout-on-next_event → DispatchSemaphore or waitForEvents: pattern on macOS runloop.

port_changes.where: src/igui/{window.rs frame_wnd_proc + child WndProcs, repl_pane.rs WM_CHAR/WM_KEYDOWN, fconsole.rs same, fedit.rs same}
port_changes.detail: Replace frame_wnd_proc switch statement (window.rs:776-820+) with NSResponder subclass overrides (keyDown:, mouseMoved:, etc.). Replace DefMDIChildProcW + GetWindowLongPtrW user storage with NSViewController userData / ivars. WM_PAINT → drawRect:. WM_SIZE → setFrameSize:. VK_* key codes → NSEvent.keyCode translation. Mouse button masks → NSEvent.type/.buttonNumber. GetKeyState modifiers → NSEvent.modifierFlags.

port_changes.what: MDI pane classes (fedit, fconsole, repl_pane, stack_view, crash_view, help_pane, doc_pane)
port_changes.where: src/igui/{fedit.rs, fconsole.rs, repl_pane.rs, stack_view.rs, crash_view.rs, help_pane.rs, doc_pane.rs}
port_changes.detail: Each pane registers a Win32 window class + WndProc; MF66 will subclass NSViewController instead. fedit text editing is currently fedit.rs; must move to NSTextView + NSTextDelegate. repl_pane split-pane layout (repl_pane.rs:9-10) → NSSplitViewController. fconsole scrollback + input line (fconsole.rs:117-135) → NSTableView (scrollback) + NSTextField (input). stack_view (stack_view.rs) table rendering → NSTableView. crash_view → NSTextView. help_pane + doc_pane (docpane crate) → Markdown rendering; can reuse docpane's layout (docpane/src/lib.rs:26-29) but feed CoreText for text measurement instead of DirectWrite.
Replace Win32 Clipboard API (OpenClipboard, GetClipboardData, SetClipboardData) with macOS NSPasteboard. CF_UNICODETEXT → NSPasteboardTypeString.
Replace Win32 SetTimer with NSTimer or DispatchSourceTimer on the main dispatch queue. WM_TIMER message becomes timer callback that posts EvTick to the event queue.

port_changes.where: src/igui/{menu.rs, tools_menu.rs, window.rs:716-735 menu installation, frame_wnd_proc WM_COMMAND}
port_changes.detail: Replace HMENU + CreateMenu/AppendMenuW with NSMenu/NSMenuItem. Keyboard accelerators (TranslateAcceleratorW, HACCEL) → NSView responder chain (keyDown: with NSEvent.keyCode filtering). WM_COMMAND handler (window.rs:817-856) → NSMenuItem target/action callbacks. Menu spec parsing (menu.rs) survives; only the Win32 menu API calls change.

SetProcessDpiAwarenessContext → macOS is always DPI-aware (NSScreen.backingScaleFactor is automatic). GetDpiForWindow → NSWindow.backingScaleFactor * 96 (DPI). Mostly just remove the Windows calls; macOS handles scaling transparently.

Window class icon (make_app_icon, window.rs:419-549) → NSImage + NSApp.applicationIconImage. Background wallpaper brush (make_logo_brush) → NSView custom drawRect: with CoreGraphics / Core Text glyphs.

Currently initializes D3D11 device + DXGI factory + Direct2D factory (renderer::install at window.rs:633). MF66 equivalent: initialize MTLDevice.makeDefaultDevice(), set up a global Metal command queue, and initialize Core Text font cache. The renderer's thread_local ctx() function (executor.rs:64-66) survives; the *contents* change from ID2D1DeviceContext to Metal command buffer state.

factor.com is Windows-only; MF66 will call MacNCL's Forth directly (in-process) instead of spawning a Factor subprocess. Remove newfactor_session.rs subprocess Stdio piping; call into wf64::Wf64Session::eval() from the worker thread. The transpiler (newfactor/transpiler.rs) may survive if MacNCL wants to accept NewFactor syntax, but the subprocess harness is gone.

Windows SEH (Structured Exception Handling) with VEH (Vectored Exception Handler) is not portable; macOS has no direct equivalent. MF66 will use Mach exception handlers or signal handlers (SIGSEGV) + landing pads. The crash_view UI (crash_view.rs) survives; only the *mechanism* for catching JIT'd code faults changes. A future port may defer this to Phase 3b.

Cooperative agents model (run_slice at wf64_ui.rs:265) is pure Rust with no OS assumptions. No porting needed — only ensure the event queue (channels::next_event) works on macOS runloop instead of Win32 message pump.

risks: Direct2D rendering is tightly coupled to D3D11 + DXGI swap chains; no abstract 'render backend' layer exists. A wholesale rewrite of renderer.rs, d3d.rs, d2d.rs is unavoidable, not refactorable.

Window message dispatch (WndProc pattern) is deeply baked into each pane class (fedit, fconsole, repl_pane, stack_view, crash_view). Porting each pane requires understanding its specific message-handler logic and translating to NSResponder/NSViewController patterns.

Feature interaction: repl_pane's D2D1HwndRenderTarget, fconsole's HwndRenderTarget, stack_view's HwndRenderTarget are all created ad-hoc per-pane and maintain their own geometry/brush cache. macOS equivalents (CAMetalLayer or MTKView) must coordinate a single Metal device + command queue; no per-HWND device allowed.

Batch execution (executor::execute) is a 'pull' model: Forth worker issues SurfaceCmd batches, GUI thread executes them. macOS Metal command encoding must happen on the render thread (or be queued via dispatch_async). The synchronization model may need adjustment.

NewFactor subprocess piping (factor_session.rs) is Windows CREATE_NO_WINDOW specific. Porting the IDE to macOS probably means dropping NewFactor support entirely and relying on in-process Forth only. Factor macro support (transpiler.rs) would need new rationale.

SEH-based crash recovery (crash_handler.rs) is the *only* safety net for JIT'd Forth faults. macOS has weaker tooling (SIGSEGV vs. SEH); implementing a robust landing-pad system is non-trivial and Phase 3b-like scope.

The xctest-derived test harness (if any) uses cfg(windows) guards; porting tests requires ensuring non-Windows builds still compile and run (at least for Rust-side unit tests; Forth tests depend on session.eval() which is platform-neutral).

Menu/accelerator architecture (tools_menu.rs, menu.rs) encodes platform-specific menu IDs (u16 MENU_CMD_BASE, etc.) and builds spec strings. The parsing survives; the menu API calls and command ID resolution change.
